target: HOL-TAXONOMY c2 — symmetric-counting acknowledgement extension + WIN-068 finding cleanup + WIN-058 finding rewrite + Not Demonstrated definition broadening + b-suffix convention documentation + Std Model Explains 'Non-discriminating' sub-pattern in legend + verdict legend reordering by evidential force + Verdict Categories block ps-summary wrapping
routed_from_iss: ISS-2287, ISS-2288, ISS-2289 (3 issues bundling 8 holes)
short_recommendation: Eight integration patches in priority order: P1 (counting-methodology paragraph generalization — Evaluation Guide), P2 (populate detail_group on Schumann/cosmological/H(r) SC clusters in data/wins.json), P3 (WIN-068 finding cleanup — move duplication sentence to detail_verdict_text), P4 (WIN-058 finding rewrite — lead with V12/V13 reconciliation), P5 (Not Demonstrated legend definition broadening to absorb registered-prospective-pending), P6 (b-suffix convention note in Part 3.1 preamble), P7 (Std Model Explains legend gets 'Non-discriminating' sub-pattern sentence), P8 (verdict legend reorder by evidential force + wrap entire Verdict Categories block in ps-summary/TLDR pattern). P2 and P7 are detail-data-and-prose pairs; P8 is the single coordinated edit combining the structural improvements that close holes 6/7/8.
kernel_of_truth: The c2 curmudgeon round is correct that the new Verdict Assignment Criteria section (generate-html.js:1757-1788) closed the three biggest c1 definitional objections — the primary-issue rule, the Misleading/Std Model decision boundary, and the no-positive-category circularity — substantively, not cosmetically. The taxonomy grade is genuinely earned at A-: definitions are clear, the primary-issue rule constrains subsequent revisions (WIN-054, WIN-043/060, WIN-023 all show the rule being applied), and the symmetric-counting acknowledgement for the tidal cluster demonstrates the standard. The remaining holes are all application-consistency gaps where the framework's good rules don't yet apply to all the WINs they cover. None requires reworking the taxonomy; all are textual or single-field data edits.
why_it_doesnt_save_the_argument: Conceptual taxonomy strength does not transfer to application consistency. Three specific gaps — the Schumann/cosmological/H(r) SC clusters acknowledging only one of four cluster groups, WIN-068 holding the last duplication-in-finding inconsistency post-cleanup, and the b-suffix convention being undocumented — are exactly the kind of process objections a careful dome defender constructs from the review's own evidence. Each is a 'you criticize me for X then do X' rhetorical opening. Closing them costs no verdict reassignments, just text and one data-field set per cluster member. The dome defender's 'counting hypocrisy' attack against the tidal cluster has been answered for one cluster of four; the other three remain open targets.

proposed_integration_patches.target_section: Evaluation Guide → Verdict Assignment Criteria → 'A note on our own counting methodology'
proposed_integration_patches.action: Replace the existing single-cluster (tidal-only) acknowledgement with a four-cluster acknowledgement. Keep the tidal cluster as the worked example and add the Schumann, cosmological, and H(r) clusters explicitly. This generalizes the symmetric-counting acknowledgement so that a dome defender cannot construct a 'counting hypocrisy' attack against any of the four observed cluster groups.
proposed_integration_patches.find: A note on our own counting methodology. We assign the same verdict structure to each WIN that the dome claims: if the dome counts five tidal constituents (M2, S2, K1, O1, N2) as five separate predictions, we evaluate each as a separate claim. Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) each receive Self-Contradicted because the dome's local moon geometry fails independently for each constituent's period. We acknowledge that all five derive from one underlying geometric impossibility — a 2,534 km moon cannot produce semidiurnal tides regardless of constituent. We apply this transparency standard symmetrically: when we note that the dome inflates its count by splitting one observation into multiple claims, the reader should know that our verdict count reflects the same granularity.
proposed_integration_patches.replace:
A note on our own counting methodology. We assign the same verdict structure to each WIN that the dome claims: if the dome counts five tidal constituents (M2, S2, K1, O1, N2) as five separate predictions, we evaluate each as a separate claim. Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) each receive Self-Contradicted because the dome's local moon geometry fails independently for each constituent's period. We acknowledge that all five derive from one underlying geometric impossibility — a 2,534 km moon cannot produce semidiurnal tides regardless of constituent.

The same pattern recurs in three other Self-Contradicted clusters whose findings share a single geometric root: Schumann cluster — WIN-002, 029, 038, 061 all derive from the dome cavity's exponential H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) producing a ~22 Hz floor rather than the observed 7.83 Hz; cosmological-bounded-geometry cluster — WIN-047, 048, 052, 054 all derive from the bounded firmament's inability to accommodate any object at z > 0; H(r)/coupling-law cluster — WIN-044, 056, 067 all derive from the published H(r) parameters producing values radically different from the observations they are nominally tested against. We count each member as the dome counts it, but the underlying logical units are fewer than the verdict count suggests. The detail_group field on each clustered WIN names its cluster; readers tracking independent failure modes should treat each named cluster as one. We apply this transparency standard symmetrically: when we note that the dome inflates its count by splitting one observation into multiple claims, the reader should know that our verdict count reflects the same granularity.
proposed_integration_patches.defender_pre_empted: The strongest dome-defender process objection on the current taxonomy is: 'You criticize me for inflating one INTERMAGNET observation into 5 WINs, then count one geometric impossibility (dome cavity → 22 Hz) as 4 self-contradictions. By your own counting standard you have 8-10 independent self-contradictions, not 20.' This patch answers that objection in advance with a stated rule that applies to all four observed cluster groups, not just the tidal one.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Populate detail_group on the three SC clusters whose members currently have detail_group = null. The values match the cluster names used in P1's prose. WIN-061 is reassigned from 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' (magnetic-broad) to 'WIN-002/029/038/061' (Schumann) because its finding leads with the dome cavity 22 Hz argument, making Schumann the primary structural failure; the magnetic-broad cluster retains 8 members (040, 041, 042, 043, 057b, 059, 060, 063) and is not visually weakened. This judgment call is surfaced in the patch notes for curmudgeon attention.

operations.note: Reassigned from magnetic-broad to Schumann. WIN-061's finding leads with the cavity 22 Hz argument (primary structural failure is the Schumann mechanism); storm phenomenology is secondary. The magnetic-broad cluster retains 8 members and is not materially weakened. Curmudgeon should confirm this boundary call.

proposed_integration_patches.structural_data_consequence: Increases distinct detail_group count from 8 to 11. The counts.groups computed at build-scripts/generate-html.js:1273 will read 11. No HTML rendering depends on the specific value beyond that counter.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Strip the trailing 'Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level.' sentence from WIN-068's finding one-liner. The same content is already present in WIN-068.detail_verdict_text's closing paragraph ('WIN-010 (BOU 2017), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), and WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble 1991–2016) all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels. The dome counts each as a separate confirmed prediction, inflating the score count without adding independent evidence.'), so no information is lost — only the visible finding-table inconsistency is closed. This aligns WIN-068 with the WIN-043/060 cleanup pattern.
proposed_integration_patches.find: Non-discriminating: the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, with modern conductivity treatment in Yamazaki & Maute 2017) together with the Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble predicts the 39-eclipse signal magnitude and geometry. The dome publishes a scaling equation (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) relevant to its 2026 forward projection (WIN-010), but the 1991–2016 Kim & Chang ensemble evaluated here attaches no dome-derived numerical prediction — it is a retrospective citation of observatory data. Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level.
proposed_integration_patches.replace: Non-discriminating: the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, with modern conductivity treatment in Yamazaki & Maute 2017) together with the Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble predicts the 39-eclipse signal magnitude and geometry. The dome publishes a scaling equation (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) relevant to its 2026 forward projection (WIN-010), but the 1991–2016 Kim & Chang ensemble evaluated here attaches no dome-derived numerical prediction — it is a retrospective citation of observatory data.
proposed_integration_patches.preserved_in: detail_verdict_text closing paragraph already states the triple-counting argument; no content loss.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Rewrite WIN-058's finding to lead with the V12/V13 reconciliation failure (the Misleading-qualifying structural problem) rather than the non-discrimination framing. The current opening — 'θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention' — describes a Std Model Explains failure mode, not a Misleading one. Per the Verdict Assignment Criteria's primary-issue rule (generate-html.js:1764), the finding one-liner should surface the structural problem that distinguishes this verdict from the adjacent SME category. The detail body and detail_verdict_text already cover the V12/V13 reconciliation, OPEN-001, and 7.3% vs sub-0.01% comparison in depth; the finding is updated to lead with the same material.
proposed_integration_patches.find: θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically. No dome prediction requires it: every distance, bearing, and timing the dome computes from θ is reproduced by standard spherical coordinates using the same WGS-84 longitude input. V13 promotes geographic longitude directly to θ, cleaning up V9's solar-noon-UTC proxy — but V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so the V13 angular identity and the V12 distance-scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation. WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–730× worse than Vincenty geodesy. Even read charitably as a model-consistency test, the identity distinguishes only the polar-azimuthal projection class — a family the dome was already committed to by its disc-centered geometry — so it cannot discriminate between the dome and any globe-based polar-azimuthal projection using the same WGS-84 inputs.
proposed_integration_patches.replace: V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so the V13 angular identity coexists with the V12 distance-scaling factor without reconciliation; the coordinate system also requires WGS-84 longitude as input (OPEN-001 unresolved) and achieves 7.3% error where Vincenty geodesy achieves sub-0.01% on the same WGS-84 inputs. The θ = −lonE identity itself is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically — so even read charitably as a model-consistency test it discriminates only the polar-azimuthal projection class the dome was already committed to.
proposed_integration_patches.verdict_unchanged: Misleading verdict is retained — the V12/V13 unreconciled coexistence + curve-fitting + WGS-84 input dependency are the Misleading-class structural problems that justify the verdict. The finding now leads with them rather than burying them behind the non-discrimination framing.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Broaden the Not Demonstrated legend definition to absorb WIN-058b (PRED-KAPPA-001: prospectively-registered prediction with methodologically suspect calibration awaiting independent verification). The current definition fits unreplicated fringe experiments (WIN-011 Mohe 1997 gravity) but does not cleanly capture registered-prospective-pending claims. The c1 holistic review flagged the same definitional narrowness; this c2 round formalizes the broadening. The new definition is the union of two arms — unreplicated-fringe-experiment and registered-prospective-pending — joined by 'OR'.
proposed_integration_patches.find: Not Demonstrated: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed.
proposed_integration_patches.replace: Not Demonstrated: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed, OR on a prospectively-registered prediction whose calibration is methodologically suspect and which awaits independent verification.
proposed_integration_patches.WIN_058b_alignment_note: After this broadening, WIN-058b's verdict (Not Demonstrated) is unambiguously inside the stated definition: PRED-KAPPA-001 is a prospectively-registered prediction, the κ = 1.677 ratio is post-hoc-fitted (methodologically suspect calibration), and the dome's own status field reports pending_independent_verification. No verdict reassignment required.

proposed_integration_patches.replace:
3.1 Verdict Summary Table

WIN numbering note. IDs with a "b" suffix (WIN-057b, WIN-058b) denote separate dome claims that share a visible numeric label on the dome's wins.html surface — the dome later added a machine-readable disambiguation block in docs/api/current/claim_index.json but the visible badges still collide. We track each as an independent WIN because each carries its own derivation, verdict, and detail prose. The "b" entry is not a refinement or sub-claim of its parent ID.

proposed_integration_patches.target_section: Verdict legend → Std Model Explains definition (line ~1661)
proposed_integration_patches.action: Augment the Std Model Explains legend definition with a sentence naming the 'Non-discriminating' sub-pattern. This surfaces the stronger form of the verdict (where the dome's prediction has zero discriminatory power between models, not merely that standard physics also predicts the same result) without splitting the category. Ten WINs (006, 010, 020, 025, 035, 036, 040, 043, 060, 068) currently lead with the word 'Non-discriminating' in their findings — this is a real sub-pattern the legend should acknowledge.
proposed_integration_patches.find: Standard Model Explains: The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve.
proposed_integration_patches.replace: Standard Model Explains: The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve. Several WINs in this category are flagged Non-discriminating: the dome's prediction does not differ in principle from the globe's prediction — failing the discriminating-prediction criterion in our Evaluation Guide.

proposed_integration_patches.action: Single coordinated edit covering Hole 7 (legend ordering by evidential force) and Hole 8 (block not progressive-disclosure wrapped). The legend is reordered Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data → Std Model Explains → Misleading → Not Demonstrated → Unfalsifiable, mapping to three failure tiers (Wrong on own terms / Not evidence for dome / Not evaluable). The entire block is wrapped in the site's standard <details><summary class="ps-summary"> + ps-tldr + ps-detail pattern so it matches every other h1-level definition block in Part 1 and gets a 2-3 sentence TLDR for skim and mobile readers. The Std Model Explains definition reflects P7's broadened wording so the two patches integrate without conflict — if P7 lands first, P8's replace text should be merged onto the new SME line; if P8 lands first, P7 still applies cleanly to the line inside the new wrapper.
proposed_integration_patches.find:
Verdict Categories Used in This Review

Refuted by Data: Direct physical measurements or experiments contradict the specific claim. Hard evidence exists proving the stated behavior does not occur or the cited source does not contain what is claimed.
Standard Model Explains: The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve.
Self-Contradicted: The dome's own stated geometry, if worked through honestly, predicts a value radically different from what the author claims. Agreement with observations is achieved only by substituting globe formulas, ignoring the dome's own geometry, or curve-fitting. See Part 2 for derivations.
Misleading: The data is misrepresented, cherry-picked, the cited values do not match the actual source, logically contradictory results are both claimed as confirmations, or the same observation is counted as multiple independent wins.
Not Demonstrated: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed.
Unfalsifiable: The claim cannot be tested by any physical measurement. Typically theological assertions.
proposed_integration_patches.replace:
Verdict Categories Used in This Review

Six categories describe distinct failure modes grouped into three tiers: Wrong on own terms (Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data), Not evidence for the dome (Standard Model Explains, Misleading), and Not evaluable (Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable). See the Verdict Assignment Criteria for the primary-issue rule and the Misleading/Std Model decision boundary.

Self-Contradicted: The dome's own stated geometry, if worked through honestly, predicts a value radically different from what the author claims. Agreement with observations is achieved only by substituting globe formulas, ignoring the dome's own geometry, or curve-fitting. See Part 2 for derivations.
Refuted by Data: Direct physical measurements or experiments contradict the specific claim. Hard evidence exists proving the stated behavior does not occur or the cited source does not contain what is claimed.
Standard Model Explains: The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve. Several WINs in this category are flagged Non-discriminating: the dome's prediction does not differ in principle from the globe's prediction — failing the discriminating-prediction criterion in our Evaluation Guide.
Misleading: The data is misrepresented, cherry-picked, the cited values do not match the actual source, logically contradictory results are both claimed as confirmations, or the same observation is counted as multiple independent wins.
Not Demonstrated: The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed, OR on a prospectively-registered prediction whose calibration is methodologically suspect and which awaits independent verification.
Unfalsifiable: The claim cannot be tested by any physical measurement. Typically theological assertions.
proposed_integration_patches.cross_patch_notes: This single replacement supersedes P5 and P7 on the same lines — the Not Demonstrated definition (P5) and the Std Model Explains 'Non-discriminating' sentence (P7) are already merged into the new wrapper. If the decider lands P5/P7 first and then P8, the find-string in P8 will fail to match because those lines have changed. Recommend the decider apply P8 BEFORE P5/P7, OR skip P5/P7 if P8 lands first (P5/P7 content is already inside P8's replace text). Alternative integration order: drop P5 and P7 as standalone patches, treat P8 as the single coordinated edit covering Holes 4, 6, 7, 8.

cluster_assignment_rationale.note: Curmudgeon should validate the WIN-061 reassignment from magnetic-broad ('WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063') to Schumann ('WIN-002/029/038/061').
cluster_assignment_rationale.arguments_for_schumann: WIN-061's finding leads with 'Dome cavity predicts ~22 Hz base frequency' — the primary structural failure is the cavity Schumann mechanism, not the storm phenomenology. The curmudgeon c2 review explicitly identifies WIN-061 as the 4th member of the Schumann SC cluster (alongside 002/029/038). Putting it in the magnetic-broad cluster obscures the Schumann root cause.
cluster_assignment_rationale.arguments_for_keeping_magnetic_broad: The magnetic-broad cluster has been settled state for some time; reassignment changes a stable boundary. WIN-061 is the only post-c1 addition to the Schumann claim space, and a defender could read the move as ad-hoc cluster boundary adjustment.
cluster_assignment_rationale.recommendation: Reassign to Schumann. The finding's leading argument is structurally determinative, and the magnetic-broad cluster still has 8 members (040-043, 057b, 059, 060, 063) — losing 061 doesn't weaken it. The boundary change is curmudgeon-reviewable.

anticipated_objections.objection: Generalizing the symmetric-counting acknowledgement weakens the headline '20 Self-Contradicted' rhetorical force.
anticipated_objections.response: It does reduce the headline force, but the trade is credibility for inflation. A sophisticated reader who notices the cluster pattern without our acknowledgement silently discounts the count and our credibility along with it. Better to lose the visible 20 → ~10 reduction in argued-effective-distinct-failures than to leave the dome defender a free counting-hypocrisy attack. The verdict counts themselves do not change — we still record 20 SC verdicts; we just label four of them as cluster-grouped.

anticipated_objections.objection: Patches P5 and P7 are made redundant by P8 because the same lines are edited.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — P8 is the coordinated single edit. P5 and P7 are kept as separable patches in case the decider wants to land legend wording changes without the structural wrapping change. The patch notes on P8 explicitly handle both integration orders (apply P8 before P5/P7, or skip P5/P7 if P8 lands).

anticipated_objections.objection: Reassigning WIN-061's detail_group changes a stable cluster boundary and could draw a 'goalpost-moved' attack.
anticipated_objections.response: The reassignment surfaces the conflict the curmudgeon already identified between WIN-061's primary mechanism (cavity 22 Hz, Schumann) and its secondary phenomenology (storm magnetic). The current boundary was set when only the magnetic broad cluster existed; introducing the Schumann cluster requires choosing which group 061 fits best, and the finding's leading argument is the determinative criterion. The patch flags the change for curmudgeon review.

anticipated_objections.objection: The b-suffix note documents the convention without fixing the underlying problem (visible badges still collide).
anticipated_objections.response: Correct — and that is the intentionally lower-risk option. Renumbering WIN-057b/058b to fresh sequential IDs would break ~30 existing internal cross-references (Part 6 sections, Kill-Shot Part 5, multiple verdict-history entries) and any external link or citation already using 057b/058b. The documentary fix is the safer path; the visible-badge collision is itself a dome-author choice (the dome ships two 'WIN-058' badges on its own wins.html) and our review's documentation of the mirror collision is honest.

holes_addressed: HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-Hole-1 (major): SC cluster inflation — P1 + P2
HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-Hole-4 (moderate): Not Demonstrated too narrow — P5 (and P8)
HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-Hole-6 (moderate): SME legend missing Non-discriminating — P7 (and P8)
HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-Hole-8 (minor): Verdict Categories h1 block not wrapped — P8
new_evidence_added: No new external evidence — all patches reuse data and arguments already present in data/wins.json and build-scripts/generate-html.js, restructuring presentation per the c2 curmudgeon recommendations.
no_verdict_changes: Zero WIN verdicts are reassigned. All 72 WIN verdicts remain as published. Two findings are rewritten (WIN-058, WIN-068) but their verdicts (Misleading, Std Model Explains) stand.

deferred_items.item: The c2 curmudgeon's deeper_analysis raised a 'hindsight rationalization' counter-consideration on the Verdict Assignment Criteria section itself — that a dome defender could argue the assignment rules were written after the assignments were made. The c2 review's response (the post-c1 WIN-054/043/060/023 changes show the rules constrain subsequent revisions) is satisfactory; no patch needed here. Flagged for awareness only.
deferred_items.action: none — included in deferred_items as documentary record

summary_for_decider: Eight patches addressing all eight HOL-TAXONOMY c2 holes. P1+P2 close the highest-leverage hole (SC cluster inflation, Hole 1) by adding three cluster names to the counting-methodology paragraph and populating detail_group on 11 cluster-member WINs. P3 closes Hole 2 with a one-sentence finding strip on WIN-068 — content already preserved in detail_verdict_text. P4 rewrites WIN-058's finding to lead with V12/V13 reconciliation; verdict unchanged. P5+P7 are pure legend wording edits (Not Demonstrated broadening, SME Non-discriminating sub-pattern). P6 adds a one-paragraph b-suffix convention note to Part 3.1 preamble. P8 is the single coordinated edit closing Holes 7+8 by reordering the verdict legend and wrapping the entire Verdict Categories block in the site-standard progressive-disclosure pattern; P8 also already merges P5 and P7's wording so the decider can land P8 standalone if preferred. The only judgment call requiring curmudgeon attention is WIN-061's reassignment from magnetic-broad to Schumann; rationale is in cluster_assignment_rationale and patch P2's note field. review_class declared holistic — multi-WIN, multi-file work touching data/wins.json + build-scripts/generate-html.js + data/sections.json across 11 cluster-member edits.
